title: cuphea calcarata
intro: Cuphea calcarata, also known as the Firecracker Plant, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Lythraceae family. It is native to Mexico and Central America, and is prized for its vibrant red and orange flowers, which resemble tiny fireworks. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves and flowers of Cuphea calcarata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections.
